verdicts.reason: The Named Snapshots feature is implemented robustly, handles edge cases like storage limits and empty input, and correctly integrates with existing functionality without introducing new correctness issues.
The Named Snapshots feature adheres to the project's established security constraints regarding local storage and markdown rendering, introducing no new attack surfaces or data exposure vectors.
The Named Snapshots feature provides clear affordances, good feedback, and handles empty/error states effectively, with previous UI concerns regarding mobile tap targets already addressed.
Named Snapshots feature is highly discoverable with clear in-app hints, keyboard shortcut documentation, and comprehensive AI-ready instructions.
Named Snapshots provide essential version control for iterative presentation design, offering a critical safety net and enabling experimentation without external tools.
verdicts.evidence: This feature solves the problem of needing to revert to previous versions or compare different design paths, which is a frequent requirement in any creative content generation. It's a common feature in robust creative tools (e.g., Figma version history, Google Docs revision history) and enhances the tool's utility beyond simple auto-save or one-time file export.

verdicts.reason: The Named Snapshots feature provides a highly differentiated and memorable version history system for a single-file markdown editor, with visual thumbnails and a convenient keyboard shortcut.
No documented lessons or architectural constraints were violated by the Named Snapshots feature. All specific requirements from the Council focus were met.
